scope: Research/design only. No source code was edited, no dependencies were installed, and no long external smoke tests were run.

executive_decision.architecture: Use a Rust Cargo workspace with a standalone local broker binary. Keep protocol, core engine, built-in addons, pi provider, and HTTP server as separate Rust crates. Keep VS Code and Obsidian clients as thin TypeScript host glue only because those platforms expose JavaScript/TypeScript extension APIs.
executive_decision.transport: MVP transport is local HTTP JSON on /health, /v1/autocomplete, /v1/cancel/:request_id, and /v1/reload, with /v1 path versioning plus an explicit protocol_version field in request/response payloads.
executive_decision.provider_strategy: Implement mock and pi providers behind one Rust provider trait. Clients never import provider crates and switch providers only through broker config.
executive_decision.main_risk: pi subprocess + model latency may be too slow for keystroke-level completion; implement idle/manual triggering first and measure before optimizing.

facts_used: Do not read tmp/, archive/, prompt-buffer.md, or unrelated ~/dev directories.
Run checkpoint before ending file-modifying sessions.
Standalone broker with thin VS Code and Obsidian clients is the core design decision.
MVP endpoints, request/response shape, pi subprocess mode, addon model, quality gates, and task order are defined there.
Hard implementation language is Rust for broker, protocol, providers, and reusable logic.
TypeScript/JavaScript is allowed only for unavoidable VS Code and Obsidian host glue.
No Rust workspace, crates, or client package scaffolding currently exists in the checked repository surface.
pi supports --mode json and --mode rpc.
pi supports --model, --thinking, --no-session, --no-tools, --no-context-files, --no-extensions, --no-skills, --no-prompt-templates, --system-prompt, and file arguments.
Pi packages/extensions execute arbitrary code, so autocomplete subprocess isolation should disable extension discovery.
--mode json emits JSONL session and event objects, not just the assistant's raw text.
Provider must extract the final assistant text from message/agent events and then parse the model-specified JSON object.
Runtime pi version checked: 0.75.5.
Help output confirms the relevant flags above exist in this environment.
axum Router supports route composition with HTTP method helpers such as get and post, and Router::with_state provides global handler state.
schemars supports deriving JsonSchema alongside serde derives and generating schemas with schema_for.
jsonschema supports one-off validation and reusable validators via validator_for, useful for fixture/schema tests.
tokio::process::Command supports async subprocess execution and kill_on_drop, but docs caution that strong guarantees require explicit kill/wait.
CancellationToken supports cancel, cancelled, clone, and child_token semantics suitable for request cancellation trees.
config-rs supports layered defaults, files, environment variables, and serde deserialization into typed structs.
globset builds a GlobSet from multiple patterns and supports is_match/matches for path privacy filters.
Native async functions in traits are not dyn-compatible; async-trait makes async trait methods usable with dyn trait objects.
clap supports derive-based command-line parsing with generated --help and --version behavior.
tower ServiceExt provides oneshot for testing services without binding a real socket.
VS Code extensions can register languages.registerInlineCompletionItemProvider.
InlineCompletionItemProvider.provideInlineCompletionItems receives TextDocument, Position, InlineCompletionContext, and CancellationToken and returns items or a thenable.
InlineCompletionItem insertText is used for preview and accept; range must begin and end on one line.
Obsidian uses CodeMirror 6 for the Markdown editor.
Obsidian plugins register CodeMirror 6 extensions with registerEditorExtension in onload.
Plugin.registerEditorExtension accepts a CodeMirror 6 Extension or array of Extensions.
Plugin.addCommand supports editor callbacks and hotkeys.
CodeMirror/Obsidian view plugins can provide decorations; widget decorations insert custom HTML elements in the editor. This is suitable for ghost text if limited to the visible viewport.

workspace_root.recommendation: Create a Cargo workspace with resolver = "2", edition = "2024", shared lint policy, and workspace dependency versions. Keep examples/fixtures outside crates so protocol fixtures are language-neutral.

responsibilities: Define serde/schemars request, response, health, cancel, reload, client, document, cursor, context, options, provider metadata, and error types.
Export JSON Schema and validate fixtures.
Provide custom semantic validation for protocol invariants that JSON Schema alone cannot express well.
public_interfaces: AutocompleteRequest, AutocompleteResponse, ErrorResponse, HealthResponse, CancelResponse, ReloadResponse
ErrorCode enum with stable snake_case wire names
Validate trait or validate_request/validate_response functions returning field-path diagnostics
schema module or export-schema binary for request/response schema files

crates.maps_plan_component: server/core engine, provider abstraction, postprocessors, mock provider
responsibilities: Define provider trait and engine request lifecycle independent of HTTP.
Implement deterministic mock provider for UI/client and server tests.
Implement postprocessing pipeline: strip markdown fences, remove repeated prefix, max character enforcement, explanation rejection, normalize newlines.
Represent cancellation, deadlines, provider errors, and normalized completion candidates.
public_interfaces: CompletionProvider trait: async complete(request, prompt, cancellation) -> ProviderResult

responsibilities: Define and implement built-in context providers, prompt templates, policy filters, and registries used by the broker.
Keep addon behavior inside the broker/core path and out of editor clients.
Enforce privacy policy before remote/pi provider calls.
public_interfaces: Built-in code and markdown prompt templates
Built-in nearby_text_window context provider that uses only explicit request context for MVP

responsibilities: Invoke pi as a subprocess with tools/session/context/extensions/skills/templates disabled for autocomplete requests.
Render strict system/user prompts, parse pi JSONL event stream, extract final assistant text, parse provider response JSON, and perform one malformed-JSON repair retry.
Implement timeout, cancellation, stdout/stderr capture, and secret redaction.
Expose no file-system or shell functionality except spawning the configured pi executable.
public_interfaces: PiProviderConfig { command, provider, model, thinking, timeout_ms, repair_retry }

responsibilities: Build the axum Router and local HTTP endpoints.
Load typed broker config and instantiate mock or pi provider by config only.
Track in-flight request cancellation tokens by request_id.
Run protocol validation, addon/context/prompt pipeline, provider, postprocessors, and response mapping.
Provide the runnable autocomplete-broker binary.
Register InlineCompletionItemProvider.
Collect prefix/suffix/cursor/document metadata and POST to the broker.
Use VS Code CancellationToken and AbortController to cancel stale requests.
Render one InlineCompletionItem from insert_text; rely on VS Code inline completion acceptance rather than provider-specific logic.
must_not_do: Import autocomplete-provider-pi or know pi command details.
Implement provider logic or postprocessing beyond minimal UI safety checks.

responsibilities: Register a CodeMirror 6 editor extension from the Obsidian Plugin.onload method.
Collect note context and call the same broker protocol.
Render ghost text with a view plugin and widget decoration.
Accept with configurable Tab/key handling and cancel stale requests on document/cursor changes.
must_not_do: Import autocomplete-provider-pi or know pi command details.
Read arbitrary vault files for provider context in the client.

runtime_http.rationale: tokio is needed for async server, timeouts, cancellation, and subprocesses. axum integrates with Tower and has simple route/state/JSON handlers for local HTTP. tower-http can provide tracing and narrow localhost CORS if Obsidian/Electron fetch requires CORS.
alternatives_rejected: actix-web: capable but less directly aligned with Tower oneshot tests for this small broker.
warp: workable but less current/conventional for new Tower-based services than axum.

serialization_schema_validation.rationale: serde/serde_json are the wire format foundation; schemars generates JSON Schema from Rust types; jsonschema validates generated schemas/fixtures in tests. Use explicit Rust validation for user-facing protocol error codes.

errors_observability.rationale: Typed library errors should be explicit and testable; broker logs need structured latency/provider diagnostics with redaction.

config_cli_privacy.rationale: config-rs handles defaults + TOML + env overrides into typed structs; clap handles broker CLI; globset enforces excluded_globs; url parses file:// document URIs before path policy checks.

subprocess_cancellation.rationale: Use async subprocesses without blocking the broker runtime. On cancellation/timeout, explicitly kill and wait for the child instead of relying only on kill_on_drop.

dynamic_provider_trait.rationale: The broker needs Arc<dyn CompletionProvider> selected by config. Native async trait methods are not dyn-compatible per async-trait docs, so use async-trait or a manual boxed-future trait. async-trait is the pragmatic MVP choice.

testing.rationale: Use fast unit tests, axum Router oneshot integration tests without sockets, fake-pi executable tests, temporary config/fixtures, and readable diffs. Avoid slow real pi tests until the dedicated runtime smoke task.

protocol_and_interfaces.wire_format: JSON using snake_case field names and #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] on request/response structs where practical.
protocol_and_interfaces.versioning: Use both /v1 endpoint paths and a required protocol_version: 1 field in request and response bodies. Reject unknown major versions with unsupported_protocol_version.
protocol_and_interfaces.request_id: Use UUIDs at the protocol boundary. Cancellation endpoint path parameter must match an in-flight request_id; cancellation is best-effort and idempotent.
validation_rules_mvp: client.name and document.language_id must be non-empty bounded strings.
cursor line/character/offset must be non-negative and internally plausible when clients can provide enough text to check.
context.prefix/context.suffix must respect configured byte limits before provider calls.
options.max_chars and deadline_ms must be bounded by broker config maximums.
insert_text returned to clients must be non-empty after postprocessing unless response status is no_suggestion/error.
protocol_and_interfaces.response_shape: Prefer one tagged response enum internally, serialized to the plan-compatible status field: ok, no_suggestion, cancelled, or error. Include metadata.latency_ms and provider_latency_ms when available.
provider_trait_contract: Provider receives an already-validated request and rendered prompt/context, not raw HTTP.
Provider may return a candidate, no_suggestion, cancelled, timeout, malformed_output, or provider_error.
Provider output is never returned directly to clients; it must pass postprocessing.
addon_contract: Context providers must declare whether they use only request-supplied context or need workspace/file access. MVP built-ins should use only request-supplied context.
PolicyEngine runs before any remote/pi provider call and can reject sensitive paths or over-budget context.
Prompt templates are selected by language_id/mode and are broker config, not client behavior.

pi_provider_design.why_stricter_than_plan: The plan disabled tools/session/context. Local pi docs also show extensions/packages can execute arbitrary code and skills/prompts can affect context, so MVP provider isolation should disable extension, skill, prompt-template, and theme discovery too.
pi_provider_design.jsonl_handling: Because pi --mode json emits event JSONL, parse line by line, ignore tool events, extract the final assistant message text from message_end/turn_end/agent_end, then parse that text as the provider_response_schema object.
pi_provider_design.repair_retry: On malformed assistant JSON, run one retry with the same isolation flags, shorter system prompt, and escaped invalid output. Do not retry timeouts or cancellations.
pi_provider_design.timeout_cancellation: Wrap child execution in tokio::select! over timeout, cancellation.cancelled(), and process completion. On timeout/cancel, kill and wait for the child, then return provider_timeout or cancelled.
pi_provider_design.secret_redaction: Before storing diagnostics, redact API-key-like tokens, Authorization headers, bearer tokens, and home-directory-local auth paths from stdout/stderr/args. Do not log full prompts by default.
pi_provider_design.model_availability_uncertainty: The MVP plan names openai/gpt-5.5. pi CLI supports --model, but this environment did not confirm that openai/gpt-5.5 is listed/available. Treat the model as config, not a hard-coded invariant; runtime smoke must verify the chosen provider/model.

verified_constraints: VS Code extension APIs are JavaScript APIs exposed to extension authors.
registerInlineCompletionItemProvider and InlineCompletionItemProvider are available in vscode.d.ts.
implementation_notes: Use TypeScript because VS Code extension host APIs are TypeScript/JavaScript.
Debounce by extension setting and cancel previous AbortController on document version, cursor, or selection change.
Build prefix/suffix from TextDocument around Position with byte/char caps matching broker config.
Return no items if the broker response request_id, document.uri, or document.version is stale.
Keep generated protocol schema/fixtures in the client package for contract tests; do not duplicate provider logic.

verified_constraints: Obsidian uses CodeMirror 6 for the Markdown editor.
Obsidian plugins can register CodeMirror 6 extensions with registerEditorExtension.
Obsidian Plugin.addCommand supports editor callbacks and hotkeys.
implementation_notes: Use TypeScript because Obsidian plugin APIs are TypeScript/JavaScript.
Use a CodeMirror ViewPlugin plus widget decoration for viewport-local ghost text.
Make Tab acceptance configurable; exact interaction with Obsidian's editor and other plugins must be runtime-verified.
Cancel stale broker requests on docChanged, selection changes, or plugin unload.
Do not read arbitrary vault files in the client; send only explicit editor context.

protocol_tests: Deserialize/serialize request and response fixtures from examples/fixtures.
Generate JSON Schema and validate fixtures with jsonschema.
Reject invalid protocol_version, missing required fields, unknown fields, overlarge context, bad max_chars, bad deadline_ms, and invalid UUIDs.
Verify stable error code wire names.
core_tests: Provider trait can be used behind Arc<dyn CompletionProvider>.
MockProvider returns deterministic suggestions for VS Code-style and Obsidian-style requests.
Postprocessor tests cover markdown fences, repeated prefix, max chars, explanations, multifile edits, and newline normalization.
CancellationToken tests cover normal completion, pre-cancelled token, and cancellation while provider awaits.
pi_provider_tests: Use fake pi executable in a tempdir/PATH; do not call real pi.
Assert argv includes --no-tools, --no-session, --no-context-files, --no-extensions, --no-skills, --no-prompt-templates, --mode json, --thinking minimal, and configured --model.
Fake JSONL success event stream yields parsed insert_text/confidence.
Malformed assistant JSON triggers exactly one repair retry.
Timeout and cancellation kill/wait for the fake child.
server_tests: Use axum Router with tower ServiceExt::oneshot for /health, /v1/autocomplete, /v1/cancel/:request_id, and /v1/reload.
Valid autocomplete with mock provider returns ok response after postprocessing.
Invalid JSON and semantically invalid requests return protocol errors.
Provider selection changes by config only.
Cancel endpoint cancels in-flight request and is idempotent for unknown/completed request_ids.
Privacy policy rejects excluded_globs before provider invocation.
client_tests: VS Code request-builder unit tests cover cursor metadata, prefix/suffix caps, settings, stale response rejection, and AbortController cancellation.
Obsidian request-builder/decorator tests cover ghost text placement, stale response rejection, and configurable accept behavior where testable.
Static checks grep or test that clients never import provider-pi or pi command strings.
smoke_tests_deferred: Real pi-backed suggestions and latency measurements are deferred to MVP-050.

red_green_refactor: Red: fixture validation test fails because types/schema do not exist.
Green: minimal protocol structs, serde, validation, and schema export pass.
Refactor: split types/errors/schema modules and write protocol README.
verifier_expectations: Generated schema validates all fixtures and rejects at least one invalid fixture.
No server/provider/client code is introduced in this slice.

implementation_order_tdd_slices.goal: Core engine, mock provider, provider trait, and postprocessors.
red_green_refactor: Red: provider trait dyn-use and postprocessor behavior tests fail.
Green: minimal async-trait provider, engine orchestration, mock provider, and postprocessors pass.
Refactor: typed errors and pipeline composition.
verifier_expectations: Tests prove provider output cannot bypass postprocessing.
Mock provider supports both client request examples.

implementation_order_tdd_slices.goal: pi provider with fake executable tests only.
red_green_refactor: Red: fake-pi argv and JSONL parsing tests fail.
Green: subprocess command construction, JSONL extraction, JSON parse, retry, timeout, cancellation, and redaction pass.
Refactor: isolate event parser and redactor for unit tests.
verifier_expectations: No real pi invocation in tests.
Timeout/cancel tests do not leave child processes behind.

implementation_order_tdd_slices.goal: Broker HTTP server, config, cancellation lifecycle, and minimal addon registries.
red_green_refactor: Red: /health and mock /v1/autocomplete integration tests fail.
Green: axum app, typed config, provider factory, in-flight cancellation map, reload endpoint, and privacy policy pass.
Refactor: separate app construction from binary CLI.
verifier_expectations: HTTP tests use in-process Router where possible.
Excluded paths are blocked before provider invocation.

implementation_order_tdd_slices.goal: Thin VS Code and Obsidian clients against mock broker.
red_green_refactor: Red: request construction/cancellation tests fail in each client package.
Green: minimal host glue displays mock ghost text and cancels stale requests.
Refactor: shared generated schema/fixtures and minimal settings.
verifier_expectations: Clients contain no provider-pi imports or pi command strings.
Tab/accept behavior is documented with any unresolved runtime caveats.

implementation_order_tdd_slices.goal: Runtime smoke with real pi provider and latency measurement.
red_green_refactor: Not a TDD implementation slice; run controlled smoke tests after mock path works.
verifier_expectations: Record median and p95 latency separately for broker overhead and provider latency.
Verify at least one VS Code and one Obsidian pi-backed suggestion or document exact blocker.

assumptions_and_uncertainties.detail: The plan names openai/gpt-5.5. CLI flags are verified, but this environment did not verify that model availability. Keep provider/model configurable and fail clearly if pi rejects the configured model.

pi help lists both flags and docs show --mode json with a prompt. The exact interaction of --print plus --mode json was not runtime-tested to avoid model calls. If --mode json already exits non-interactively, --print may be redundant.
Local docs say --mode json emits session/event JSONL. The provider must not expect raw provider_response_schema on stdout.

VS Code inline completion API and InlineCompletionItem insert/preview behavior are verified from vscode.d.ts. The extension should avoid custom Tab binding unless default behavior fails in smoke tests.

assumptions_and_uncertainties.topic: Obsidian ghost text and Tab handling
assumptions_and_uncertainties.detail: Obsidian/CM6 extension and decoration mechanisms are source-backed. Exact Tab acceptance behavior and conflicts with core editor/plugins require MVP-040/MVP-050 runtime verification.
The broker should include narrow localhost CORS support because Obsidian runs web technology in Electron, but the exact fetch/CORS behavior was not verified here.
Crate choices were checked against current docs/search results on 2026-05-28. Implementation workers should use cargo add/current compatible versions and adjust minor API details as needed.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: pi subprocess and model latency make completions too slow.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Use idle/manual trigger defaults, keep mock provider for UI work, expose provider behind trait/config, and measure in MVP-050 before optimizing.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Model returns malformed JSON or explanatory text.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Strict prompt, parse assistant text only, one repair retry, postprocessing rejection, and tests for malformed outputs.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Autocomplete requests accidentally load pi context, tools, extensions, or skills.
Sensitive paths/context leak to remote provider.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: PolicyEngine with excluded_globs, remote_context_byte_limit, no implicit file reads in MVP context providers, and tests that policy runs before provider invocation.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Clients diverge from protocol or duplicate provider logic.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Generated schema/fixtures, client contract tests, and verifier grep/tests forbidding provider-pi imports and pi command strings.

handoff_for_next_workers: MVP-010 should start by creating the Cargo workspace and protocol crate exactly enough to validate fixtures and export JSON Schema.
Do not implement HTTP server or pi provider in MVP-010.
MVP-020 should rely on this handoff's fake-pi JSONL insight; parsing raw stdout as provider JSON would be wrong for --mode json.
MVP-030 should ensure all provider selection is config-only and all privacy policy checks happen before provider invocation.
MVP-040 should keep TypeScript small and host-specific; all reusable logic stays in Rust unless platform APIs make that impossible.
